children.heading: Cave
children.label: 1 Cave
children.text: The term “cave” means any naturally occurring void, cavity, recess, or system of interconnected passages which occurs beneath the surface of the earth or within a cliff or ledge (including any cave resource therein, but not including any vug, mine, tunnel, aqueduct, or other manmade excavation) and which is large enough to permit an individual to enter, whether or not the entrance is naturally formed or manmade. Such term shall include any natural pit, sinkhole, or other feature which is an extension of the entrance.

children.heading: Federal lands
children.label: 2 Federal lands
children.text: The term “Federal lands” means lands the fee title to which is owned by the United States and administered by the Secretary of Agriculture or the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Indian lands
children.label: 3 Indian lands
children.text: The term “Indian lands” means lands of Indian tribes or Indian individuals which are either held in trust by the United States for the benefit of an Indian tribe or subject to a restriction against alienation imposed by the United States.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 4 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” means any Indian tribe, band, nation, or other organized group or community of Indians, including any Alaska Native village or regional or village corporation as defined in, or established pursuant to, the Alaska Native Claims settlement Act ( et seq.).143 U.S.C. 1601So in original. Probably should be capitalized.1

children.heading: Cave resource
children.label: 5 Cave resource
children.text: The term “cave resource” includes any material or substance occurring naturally in caves on Federal lands, such as animal life, plant life, paleontological deposits, sediments, minerals, speleogens, and speleothems.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 6 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture or the Secretary of the Interior, as appropriate.

children.heading: Speleothem
children.label: 7 Speleothem
children.text: The term “speleothem” means any natural mineral formation or deposit occurring in a cave or lava tube, including but not limited to any stalactite, stalagmite, helictite, cave flower, flowstone, concretion, drapery, rimstone, or formation of clay or mud.

children.heading: Speleogen
children.label: 8 Speleogen
children.text: The term “speleogen” means relief features on the walls, ceiling, and floor of any cave or lava tube which are part of the surrounding bedrock, including but not limited to anastomoses, scallops, meander niches, petromorphs and rock pendants in solution caves and similar features unique to volcanic caves.
